perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) Two women wearing white safety helmets and reflective vests are in a retail store under construction; one points towards a shelf while talking. (2) Quick cuts show one woman placing items on high shelves, another cutting open a cardboard box with a utility knife, and one standing near a tall ladder supervising work behind a plastic sheet. (3) Transition to a woman standing in front of storage bins saying something to the camera while wearing her vest over a green dress. (4) A series of clips show them playfully 'working': hanging clothes and immediately moving them, carrying a box around the store without purpose, and laughing while applying a tiny sticker to a counter. (5) The video ends with a static graphic of a logo featuring a cartoon woman and event details.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) Upbeat, medium-tempo acoustic guitar and piano music begins. A female voice speaks in a bright, narrative tone. (2) The music continues as the primary soundscape; occasional subtle ambient rustling of boxes and movement is heard under the BGM. (3) Music shifts slightly in tone as a female voice says 'I am ready' in Mandarin. (4) The BGM becomes more playful and whimsical with woodwind-like synth sounds. Frequent sounds of female laughter and light-hearted chatter occur. (5) The music reaches a final chord and fades out as the screen transitions to the end card.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) '你以為的快閃進櫃' - Hook text indicating expectation of pop-up store setup, serious tone. (2) '擺設討論', '商品上架', '包材處理', '水電設備監工', '商品數量清點' - Functional text describing various professional setup tasks. (3) '實際上' - Large text indicating the shift to reality. (4) '裝忙', '拿起來又放回去', '這邊搬搬那邊搬搬', '我盡我小小的心力', '太小了吧' - Decorative text mocking their own lack of productivity with a humorous tone. (5) 'TAIPEI POP UP STORE DREAM PLAZA 7/25~9/7' - Functional text providing event dates and location.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Intro (Expectation) -> Reality Shift -> Humorous Montage -> Event Info

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 2 main female creators, 1 male worker visible on a ladder in the background during the 'supervision' scene.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: Two Asian women; one has long dark hair, wearing a white helmet, yellow reflective vest, and a green dress or black shorts set at different times.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A retail environment with wooden shelves, cardboard boxes, clothing racks with children's apparel, and a backroom storage area with labeled plastic bins.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video uses a 'Expectation vs. Reality' trope to humorously promote an upcoming 'Haha Baby' pop-up store at Dream Plaza. By contrasting the serious, hard-working imagery of construction with the lighthearted, semi-productive 'reality' of the creators, it builds a relatable and fun brand image while informing viewers of the event dates and location.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The video focuses entirely on the preparation of a specific branded store and ends with an informational card regarding the store's opening.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Usage of 'Expectation vs. Reality' text overlays and variety-show style editing.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video targets a family or lifestyle audience interested in children's products and the influencer's personality. The contrast in outfits (safety gear vs. stylish dress) serves as a visual hook.
